122781.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women, that is. In such a government, all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

122782.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk wants a permanent youth council, monthly neighborhood office hours by civil servants, and better awareness of existing initiatives (participation platform). Everyone in Rijswijk should be able to participate and co-decide through resident tables and the right to challenge.

122785.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk wants a pleasant and vibrant city center, both in the old center, the Bogaard, and the neighborhoods. To this end, we want to give hospitality entrepreneurs as much freedom as possible within certain limits. After all, we must also take into account the livability in the neighborhood.

122788.explanation: We will continue to take responsibility for the reception of refugees in our asylum seekers' center from humanity and solidarity. Reception goes hand in hand with language education and participation in work or volunteer work, so newcomers can participate and contribute. This way, we give people in need a home in Rijswijk.

122793.explanation: The regular free collection of bulky waste mainly benefits those who use it frequently, such as contractors. The current system, where a free mobile environmental station is set up in a neighborhood once a month, offers most Rijswijk residents the opportunity to dispose of their bulky waste for free.

122794.explanation: In Rijswijk's green areas such as the Landgoederenzone, Wilhelminapark, and Elsenburgerbos, the noise standards are already very strict. At event locations such as near the Naald, GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk believes that conviviality, connection, and a vibrant city should be central.

122795.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk aims for an ambitious local inclusion agenda. Part of this is that we become an accessible municipality for people with physical disabilities. They should also have unhindered access to non-municipal buildings. The municipality must support entrepreneurs in this.

122796.explanation: People with social assistance benefits in Rijswijk can already use the Ooievaarspas. GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk believes every child should be able to play sports. We support existing initiatives, such as Happy Fit and the Movement Broker. We raise the income limit from 130% to 150% of the social minimum.

122799.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk wants music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. With an Ooievaarspas scheme, we ensure that every child can receive music lessons.

122801.explanation: Our monument offerings in Rijswijk are beautiful and unique, such as the Old Center, De Naald, Wilhelminalantaarn, and the Museum for Population Protection, etc. These must be cherished. GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk believes that proper care is needed. This is not possible without sufficient budget.

122802.explanation: It is very important to feel safe on the streets in Rijswijk. More cameras are not the right solution. Privacy is a great value. Therefore, we choose more enforcement officers on the street who can intervene if something goes wrong, especially for women, LGBTIQ+ people, and people wearing headscarves.

122804.explanation: Municipal enforcement officers already have enough resources and mandate to perform their work. GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk prefers youth work, neighborhood work, and good contacts with neighborhood watch and police to keep our neighborhoods safe.

122805.explanation: With starter loans, public money is used to support people buying a home. GL-PvdA Rijswijk believes that money can be better invested in building affordable housing. It creates more inequality and does not help lower housing prices.

122808.explanation: Houses for the people, not for profit. In this period, the municipality, at the initiative of GroenLinks-PvdA among others, has introduced an owner-occupancy obligation. We preferably want to extend this with a longer term and other appropriate and legally feasible measures against housing speculation.

122811.explanation: Social assistance is a right; you do not have to earn it. GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk takes the subsistence security of our residents very seriously. People on social assistance deserve a stable base, an affordable home, and trust from the municipality. Extra requirements undermine mutual solidarity.

122813.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk supports generous policies to assist households. We want to raise the income limit for minimum arrangements from 130% to 150% of the Legal Social Minimum.

122815.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk wants more shared cars and therefore also to strategically establish charging points. With this, GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk wants to give more space back to the people in the city.

122818.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk wants to introduce low-emission zones first on the major access roads, such as Beatrixlaan. Later, we will also establish zero-emission zones in areas including Oud-Rijswijk and the Bogaard. We also ensure clean alternatives through our mobility policy.

122819.explanation: Culture-sensitive care is part of inclusive and accessible care. This includes culture-sensitive services and facilities. The municipality of Rijswijk already practices this, having become a dementia-friendly municipality at the initiative of GL-PvdA Rijswijk.

122820.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and care are huge. You do not solve this by cutting more or always choosing the cheapest option. GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk believes we must mainly ensure certainty and provide help that matches the concerns of our youth.

122821.explanation: In recent years, the number of menstrual product distribution points (mups) has already been expanded, including in schools and municipal buildings. GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk wants to expand this offering and strengthen cooperation with partners, such as the Food Bank.

122822.explanation: To cover municipal expenses, we will increase the property tax by the consumer price index plus a maximum of 15%. We do this for broad prosperity: quality of life comes first. Health, a clean and safe environment, good care, and having a good roof over one's head are priorities.

122824.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk stands for fair cost distribution, a livable city, and sustainable use of public space. Tourist tax is not a punishment but a means to ensure that visitors co-invest in the city. For now, it does not need to increase.

122827.explanation: The municipality continues to give priority to status holders with residence permits for social rental housing. This takes place in combination with building additional social rental homes, so waiting times for other housing seekers do not increase.

122829.explanation: Space in Rijswijk is scarce and the supply of coffee shops in neighboring municipalities is large. GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk chooses to use the remaining available real estate for social facilities such as general practitioner practices, meeting spaces, or libraries.

122832.explanation: As far as GL-PvdA Rijswijk is concerned, we will encourage shared housing. Renting rooms to up to two persons will be deregulated. Fewer rules will apply for in-house and hospitation rental, provided this does not lead to undesirable situations. We will continue to monitor and enforce against abuses with the Pand Brigade and the Rent Team.

122835.explanation: The center will become low-traffic. GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk believes the center should become an attractive area for shopping and staying. This may mean that parking spaces have to be sacrificed for, for example, green areas or terraces.

122836.explanation: The center will become low-traffic. We will address the area around Herenstraat and the pedestrian and bicycle tunnel under Haagweg. We will keep the tunnel open and renovate it. Various groups will be invited to think along and, if possible, cooperate.

122837.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk chooses to invest in prevention through youth work and youth activities. Enforcement officers have sufficient resources and mandate to tackle nuisance. Moreover, more repression leads to a waterbed effect, where offenders move to other locations.

122839.explanation: The Rijswijk Museum is a cultural attraction. GroenLinks-PvdA Rijswijk invests in a strong cultural foundation. This means structural support for institutions such as Trias, the library, and Museum Rijswijk, and a subsidy policy that allows room for continuity and innovation.

122781.explanation: Better for Rijswijk chooses knowledge and experience.

122782.explanation: In Rijswijk, this can be done through a referendum.

122785.explanation: Consideration must be given to the surroundings. For example, BvR can imagine making separate agreements for night openings in residential areas.

122788.explanation: There was a promise from the state secretary. Better for Rijswijk believes that 9 years of reception, despite the "compulsion law," is sufficient. It's time to pay attention to the homeless and roofless.

122793.explanation: If financial space becomes available, that is an important wish. Currently, it is already possible to bring bulky waste free of charge 12 times to Avalex and twice a year at a central place in the neighborhood.

122794.explanation: There are standards that organizations must adhere to. Occasionally, limited deviations should be possible.

122795.explanation: It is important to make every building accessible to all audiences. If there is financial space for this, then a contribution from the municipality should also be allowed.

122796.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in at least one sport. The Ooievaarspas must provide that opportunity.

122799.explanation: Subsidies should be channeled through, for example, Trias.

122802.explanation: Many hotspots. Don't wait too long. Everyone should feel safe.

122804.explanation: Enforcers are too often assaulted. Carrying a baton commands respect.

122805.explanation: Extend the scheme to cover all of Rijswijk.

122808.explanation: This prevents unscrupulous landlords from entering the market.

122811.explanation: Volunteering can be a first step towards paid employment.

122813.explanation: A limited increase should be negotiable, as income fluctuations sometimes leave people falling through the cracks.

122815.explanation: The number of places must be increased due to the rise in electric cars.

122818.explanation: The fleet is becoming increasingly sustainable due to government measures. Maintain the current measures. Entrepreneurs must eventually switch to cleaner means of transport.

122819.explanation: Faith should play no role in good care.

122821.explanation: A small budget to distribute menstrual products through free outlets (food bank or giveaway shop) is desirable.

122822.explanation: The municipality should always look for opportunities to keep the property tax (ozb) as low as possible. If house prices rise, lower the ozb rate. Also consider subsidies from the national or European government that could help. Increase ozb only with inflation correction, if necessary.

122824.explanation: Tourists are welcome and should not pay excessive additional taxes.

122827.explanation: This group should observe the same waiting period as starters, i.e., wait their turn.

122832.explanation: A single room, for example for students, should be allowed. Landlords (such as employment agencies) should be restricted as much as possible.

122835.explanation: Due to reserved spots (electric charging stations and Greenwheels), additional spaces are welcome.

122836.explanation: Currently not negotiable without new parking space.

122837.explanation: A lot of nuisance from cyclists and mopeds.

122839.explanation: The museum is important for Rijswijk and should be able to survive with fewer paid staff and an improved program. The subsidy can then remain the same.

122781.explanation: We look at knowledge, ability, and availability. There should be no distinction. If there are more women than men involved, we have no problem with that.

122782.explanation: This option already exists and should be maintained.

122785.explanation: At present, there is a good policy that fits the various cafes and restaurants.

122788.explanation: The contract with the COA for the Rijswijk asylum seeker center ends in 2027. We believe that housing economic migrants/asylum seekers should end then. Rijswijk has already contributed a lot and faces major challenges in housing, livability, and facilities. We say no to an asylum center.

122793.explanation: CERTAINLY! At least twice a year. This is also in the Rijswijks-Belang program. Residents already pay enough waste disposal charges!

122794.explanation: There are not that many festivals and parties in Rijswijk; the existing rules are already quite strict. That's how Rijswijks-Belang wants to keep it.

122795.explanation: Not a task for the municipality but for the entrepreneurs themselves.

122796.explanation: There are good arrangements such as the Ooievaarspas, which offers substantial discounts to people on welfare benefits.

122799.explanation: No, it must remain a choice. It is also unaffordable. Music lessons can also be organized at schools. Rijswijk has the Trias Music School, which receives about one million in subsidies (more than enough).

122801.explanation: Depends on the 'maintenance obligation' the national government imposes on monument owners.

122802.explanation: Especially at known hotspots for illegal dumping and places with high crime (burglary, drugs, vandalism, theft, violent incidents, and violent crimes).

122804.explanation: Fine, also to use. Authority and respect must return. The baton should be used, in our view, during disturbances (for example, highway blockades).

122805.explanation: Not a task for the municipality. The municipality is not a bank.

122808.explanation: This is the normal procedure, with exceptions for recognized private rent.

122811.explanation: Every resident of Rijswijk should be able to develop themselves and participate in our society. This includes guidance towards work or meaningful day activities, language skills, digital skills, and combating loneliness.

122813.explanation: No, it's fine as it is.

122815.explanation: Better to have more parking spaces for all cars. The parking standard of 0.6 per household should be increased. We aim for a parking standard of 1.1, equal to car ownership in Rijswijk.

122818.explanation: Not as long as there are insufficient alternatives to replace delivery and/or supply vans.

122819.explanation: Care is Care. We live here in the Netherlands. There should be no distinction, not even among healthcare providers.

122820.explanation: We will stop labeling our youth. The influx into youth care is rising too fast. Many young people do not need intensive care. We focus on prevention, early detection, and cooperation with schools and general practitioners.

122821.explanation: Not a task for the municipality; there are good initiatives from welfare organizations for this.

122822.explanation: We already pay so much tax.

122824.explanation: It is indeed a tool to work with, but unfortunately some of our hotels in Rijswijk function as shelters for homeless people, Ukrainians, and status holders. Because of this, city marketing has been at a standstill for some time.

122827.explanation: No more automatic priority for status holders. This is no longer mandatory nationally, but Rijswijk has continued to grant priority until now. There are 9,000 Rijswijk residents on a housing waiting list-help them first.

122832.explanation: Then you would never be able to live in student housing.

122835.explanation: We have enough, including garages and both above-ground and underground parking spaces.

122836.explanation: The car is a frequently used means of transport in Rijswijk, so leave it as is.

122837.explanation: That should happen following a motion adopted by Rijswijks-Belang.

122839.explanation: Certainly. The “museum” receives over €450,000 in subsidy, part of which is used for salaries. Rijswijk does not have a museum but a Tollenhuis, not comparable to a museum.

122781.explanation: Rijswijk residents benefit more from a solid, visible, and nearby executive board than from a strict male/female quota. We choose quality, diverse backgrounds, and attention to neighborhoods-not ticking boxes.

122782.explanation: Residents must be able to participate in and co-decide on major choices about our city. A referendum can be part of this, if it is clear beforehand what it concerns and what will happen with the results.

122785.explanation: Hospitality venues should be given space, but not at the expense of sleep and peace in the street. Opening hours must be determined in consultation with entrepreneurs and residents, appropriate to the neighborhood.

122788.explanation: Rijswijk must do its part, but not more than the city can handle. Shelters must be decent for fleeing people and the neighborhood, with clear agreements about numbers, duration, and safety.

122793.explanation: If bulky waste collection costs money or is complicated, it more easily ends up on the street. Free and easy curbside pickup helps keep neighborhoods clean and prevents litter near containers and on sidewalks.

122794.explanation: Parties and events belong to a lively city but should not cause nuisance in your living room. The standard should not automatically become stricter but better enforced and fairly weighed per location.

122795.explanation: Many buildings in Rijswijk remain difficult for people with disabilities. Entrepreneurs are often left to manage alone. The municipality can co-fund adjustments so more buildings become simply usable for everyone.

122796.explanation: Sports are healthy and help against loneliness. Membership fees are often too expensive for people on welfare. The municipality can reimburse sports memberships so children and adults can simply participate.

122799.explanation: Music lessons give children enjoyment, confidence, and opportunities, even if there is little money at home. With municipal support, every child who wants can take music lessons, not just those who can afford it.

122801.explanation: Rijswijk is changing rapidly. Precisely then it is important to be careful with buildings and places that tell our story. Monuments are not a luxury but part of the face and memory of the city.

122802.explanation: Rijswijk residents must feel safe on the street. In places with a lot of nuisance, familiar faces and cameras belong. Cameras are no magic solution but can help catch offenders and restore peace.

122804.explanation: Rijswijk must be safe for residents and enforcement officers. If they face aggression more often, a baton is justifiable, provided there is good training, clear rules, and an emphasis on de-escalation.

122805.explanation: Starters hardly get a chance in Rijswijk now. With a targeted starter loan, well integrated into the rules, we give young Rijswijkers and movers a fair chance at their own home again.

122808.explanation: Rijswijk homes are meant to be lived in, not hoarded. Anyone who buys a house here must live in it themselves. This way, we give ordinary Rijswijkers more opportunities and curb investors.

122811.explanation: People with benefits must get a fair chance to participate. Socially useful work or volunteer work can help, as long as it fits someone's health and situation and is a step toward regular work.

122813.explanation: More and more Rijswijkers struggle to make ends meet, also just above the minimum. By raising the income limit, these groups get help sooner, preventing debts and stress in the neighborhood.

122815.explanation: Rijswijk must enable electric driving but also carefully manage scarce parking space and green areas. Therefore, we want to expand charging points where it logically fits, step by step and in consultation with the neighborhoods.

122818.explanation: Many people walk in the center, and the air is often polluted. A targeted environmental zone can help keep polluting vehicles away. This can only happen with clear rules, good alternatives, and time for residents to adjust.

122819.explanation: If you need care, you must feel safe and understood. Where possible, residents should be able to choose a healthcare provider that matches their background. The municipality will monitor quality and accessibility.

122820.explanation: Youth care is about good help at the right time, not the lowest price. The municipality must mainly focus on quality, continuity, and what families in Rijswijk truly experience from the guidance.

122821.explanation: No one should be without sanitary products due to lack of money. But instead of free distribution for everyone, we address poverty more broadly and ensure help with financial worries is faster and better available.

122822.explanation: Housing costs in Rijswijk are heavy for many people. If financially possible, the property tax should be lowered or at least not increased further. Meanwhile, it remains important to maintain neighborhoods, sports, and green spaces.

122824.explanation: Rijswijk is not a tourist city like Amsterdam or Rotterdam. Tourist tax should not automatically rise or fall but fairly reflect the actual costs the municipality incurs.

122827.explanation: Rijswijk must do what is socially responsible. Status holders already have a right to housing, while many Rijswijkers have been waiting for years. Extra priority can no longer be explained to people on the waiting list.

122829.explanation: Rijswijk is already a full city. A coffeeshop does not fit what many Rijswijkers find important: safe, quiet neighborhoods and a center that invites living, shopping, and sports. We choose livability and safety in the neighborhood here.

122832.explanation: In residential neighborhoods, there should be ordinary homes, not disguised room rentals with constantly changing residents. The municipality must be strict where room rentals cause nuisance and pressure on the neighborhood.

122835.explanation: Space in the center is limited. We do not want large new parking lots, but visitors should be able to park easily in garages and on the street, without harming livability and green spaces.

122836.explanation: All centers must remain easily accessible, especially for less mobile residents of Rijswijk and entrepreneurs. At the same time, we want less shortcut and through traffic.

122837.explanation: Bogaard city center must feel safe and pleasant for visitors, entrepreneurs, and residents. That is why we want more visible enforcement against nuisance, with clear boundaries and quick follow-up on reports.

122839.explanation: The Rijswijk Museum tells the story of our city and connects young and old with art, heritage, and education. Especially in a growing city, culture and history must remain accessible.

122781.explanation: D66 Rijswijk wants the city council to consist of an equal number of men and women. People with the right qualifications should have equal opportunities to become aldermen. It can be done - vote D66!

122782.explanation: D66 Rijswijk believes residents should have more influence on important decisions. Through the platform 'Samen.Rijswijk' and meetings, residents can join the conversation. This reduces the distance between residents and the municipality. It can be done - vote D66!

122785.explanation: D66 Rijswijk wants a lively and vibrant Rijswijk and believes cafés and restaurants should be free to choose their opening hours. Entrepreneurs must respect their neighbors. All interests must be weighed. It can be done - vote D66!

122788.explanation: D66 Rijswijk is against closing the asylum reception center. Municipalities must shelter asylum seekers; we adhere to national agreements and the distribution law. It can be done - vote D66!

122793.explanation: Free collection of bulky waste prevents littering and dumping elsewhere. This keeps Rijswijk cleaner. It can be done - vote D66!

122794.explanation: D66 Rijswijk wants a vibrant Rijswijk with fun festivals while ensuring livability for local residents. Current noise standards are sufficient and should not be tightened if there is no nuisance. It can be done - vote D66!

122795.explanation: Entrepreneurs must comply with legal accessibility requirements for their buildings themselves. The municipality does not pay for this but does make its own buildings accessible to people with disabilities. It can be done - vote D66!

122796.explanation: D66 Rijswijk finds it important that everyone can participate in sports, including those with low incomes. Through the Ooievaarspas, people receiving social assistance can join a sports club. D66 Rijswijk will maintain this scheme. It can be done - vote D66!

122799.explanation: D66 Rijswijk wants all children to have access to music, singing, and dance at school. This belongs to a rich school day. This way all children can develop their talents. It can be done - vote D66!

122801.explanation: D66 Rijswijk finds it important to showcase Rijswijk’s history. That is why D66 supports the annual Open Monument Day and ensures that the municipality opens its own buildings. It can be done - vote D66!

122802.explanation: The number of crimes is decreasing and most residents (80%) feel safe in their neighborhood. Therefore, D66 Rijswijk believes camera surveillance should be used sparingly. Expansion of camera surveillance only happens if absolutely necessary. It can be done - vote D66!

122804.explanation: D66 Rijswijk is cautious about this but simultaneously sees street behavior becoming more aggressive. Community enforcement officers (BOA's) may carry batons if well trained and if necessary for their work. The safety of BOA's is important. It can be done - vote D66!

122805.explanation: D66 Rijswijk finds it important that young people can make a start in the housing market. D66 Rijswijk wants to make the starter loan for young people possible again. This allows first-time buyers to borrow money from the municipality. This helps them purchase a home. Vote D66.

122808.explanation: D66 Rijswijk believes houses are meant to be lived in. Investors may not buy up new homes or rental properties solely to rent them out. D66 prevents this through an owner-occupancy obligation. The housing inspection team ensures compliance. It can be done - vote D66!

122811.explanation: D66 Rijswijk opposes mandatory work. However, D66 wants people receiving social assistance to be able to do part-time volunteer work without losing income. Volunteer work helps people participate in society. It can be done - vote D66!

122813.explanation: Poverty still exists in Rijswijk, limiting some people's opportunities to participate in society. D66 Rijswijk wants to fight poverty and focuses on early detection of problems. Raising the income threshold helps in this. It can be done - vote D66!

122815.explanation: D66 Rijswijk wants to encourage sustainable driving. Therefore, the municipality must designate more parking spots as charging stations. This enables more people to switch to sustainable transportation. It can be done - vote D66!

122818.explanation: D66 Rijswijk supports an environmental zone to exclude polluting vehicles from the city center. This contributes to cleaner air and a healthier city. It can be done - vote D66!

122819.explanation: D66 Rijswijk stands for equal treatment of everyone, regardless of origin, religion, age, disability, or sexual orientation. Choosing care providers based on religion, culture, or beliefs does not fit within that. It can be done - vote D66!

122820.explanation: It is important that children receive quick and good help. Price matters, but quality comes first. D66 Rijswijk chooses effective youth care, not the cheapest. It can be done - vote D66!

122821.explanation: Not everyone can afford menstrual products. D66 Rijswijk wants the municipality to provide free tampons and sanitary pads. This ensures everyone can participate in society. It can be done - vote D66!

122822.explanation: D66 Rijswijk does not want to reduce the property tax (ozb), but ensures the tax does not increase too much. The property tax may not rise more than inflation. This keeps housing affordable. It can be done - vote D66!

122824.explanation: D66 Rijswijk does not want to increase the tourist tax. The tax should remain comparable to neighboring municipalities. Rijswijk wants to stay attractive to tourists. It can be done - vote D66!

122827.explanation: D66 Rijswijk believes refugees with a residence permit should be prioritized for their first rental home. This allows the municipality to fulfill its legal duty to house refugees. Afterwards, the same rules apply to them as to everyone else. It can be done - vote D66!

122829.explanation: D66 Rijswijk is not in favor of a coffeeshop in the municipality. But if an entrepreneur meets all legal requirements, establishment is permitted. It can be done - vote D66!

122832.explanation: D66 Rijswijk does not want a ban on room rentals. In certain cases, this can be a good temporary solution. However, D66 tackles illegal room rentals via the housing inspection team, which checks that everything complies with the rules. It can be done - vote D66!

122835.explanation: D66 Rijswijk does not want new parking spots in the center. Existing parking spots will be better organized: underground or in garages. Public parking areas will get green, water-permeable paving. It can be done - vote D66!

122836.explanation: D66 Rijswijk wants a car-free center. Cars can park in garages and underground parking. Residents and entrepreneurs from Rijswijk can still park their cars. This makes the center more pleasant. It can be done - vote D66!

122837.explanation: In case of nuisance in Bogaard city center, appropriate action must be taken. D66 Rijswijk wants enforcers to be present and intervene when necessary. This keeps the center safe and pleasant for everyone. It can be done - vote D66!

122839.explanation: D66 Rijswijk does not want to cut funding to the Rijswijk Museum. Culture and museums are important for the city. D66 Rijswijk finds it important to support multiple museums, such as the Rijswijk Museum and the Civil Defense Museum. It can be done - vote D66!

122781.explanation: People governing the municipality must have good qualities. Gender, orientation, or religion do not matter.

122782.explanation: No complicated fuss with referenda. Good participation of Rijswijk residents ensures good decisions.

122785.explanation: Opening hours are the entrepreneur’s own choice. Closing times are agreed upon with entrepreneurs.

122788.explanation: The current asylum reception center will close in 2027 as agreed. We comply with national laws such as the Distribution Act. Rijswijk is growing and has little space. Therefore, we want available space to be more considered. We want more balance in reception in the region.

122793.explanation: The polluter pays. It is not intended that all residents of Rijswijk contribute to collecting others’ waste. Free disposal at the recycling center is always possible. Twice a year, a container is placed in each neighborhood for free bulky waste disposal.

122794.explanation: We want more space for festivals, events, and parties. It is the organizer’s own responsibility to comply with legal rules. If there is nuisance, we take action.

122795.explanation: Entrepreneurs and owners of commercial buildings are legally responsible for the accessibility of their own buildings.

122796.explanation: Social assistance is a stepping stone to work and reintegration into society. People receiving social assistance are not helped by various disconnected rules or money for sports equipment. That only leads to more confusion and does not solve any problems.

122799.explanation: Culture is important. Trias or another Rijswijk organization can receive funding from the municipality for this. We would like schools to take on this responsibility themselves.

122801.explanation: We cherish Rijswijk's cultural and historical heritage. We want the municipality to take care of this and promote our heritage to visitors and Rijswijk residents, such as the Naald, Museum Rijswijk, Museum Bescherming Bevolking, and the Landgoederenzone.

122802.explanation: We deploy (mobile) camera surveillance more quickly where necessary, also in known locations where illegal dumping occurs. Camera surveillance increases the chance of catching offenders, offers monitoring of risk areas, and enhances the feeling of safety for Rijswijk residents.

122804.explanation: Under strict conditions, upon request from the enforcement officers themselves, and for high-risk deployments. It is important that our public order and safety officers (boa’s) can act with the right tools to do their job properly.

122805.explanation: A home is the foundation for a happy life. We support people who want to buy a house for the first time with a starter loan and advocate for the construction of sufficient affordable housing.

122808.explanation: Rijswijk currently builds and will build enough affordable homes. If a house is rented out, we see no problem with that. We will tackle illegal renting vigorously with strict enforcement.

122811.explanation: Social assistance is a stepping stone to work and reintegration into society. For those who cannot (any longer) work, options such as a tailored workplace or meaningful daytime activities will be explored. Those who do not want to contribute may have their benefits reduced.

122813.explanation: Rijswijk residents who need help should receive it. We view assistance with poverty more broadly than just from the municipality alone. We oppose superficial solutions that only address symptoms of poverty instead of the underlying problems that cause or sustain it.

122815.explanation: We believe the use of electric cars is good for air quality in Rijswijk. Charging stations will only be installed where there is sufficient demand in the neighborhoods.

122818.explanation: We are in favor of a cleaner city and good air quality. Rijswijk already has environmental zones. We are cautious about introducing zero-emission zones.

122819.explanation: Our starting point is that patients and healthcare providers decide together on appropriate care and wellbeing. With individual control and responsibility, we make healthcare future-proof.

122820.explanation: We focus on controlling costs of youth care in Rijswijk. The rising costs demand targeted action. We emphasize prevention and are careful and effective in the use of more intensive forms of care.

122821.explanation: People in poverty are not helped by disconnected rules or free products. That only leads to more confusion and does not structurally solve poverty. We do see a role here for schools, employers, and the national government.

122822.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is not intended to raise extra money. An increase in property tax affects all Rijswijk residents. Therefore, we are extra cautious about raising the property tax.

122824.explanation: We want low taxes. Increasing the tourist tax hits people in the wallet and drives away (hotel) guests. Rijswijk is a hospitable municipality. Therefore, we oppose an increase.

122827.explanation: We provide temporary housing for status holders, but they do not receive priority for social rental housing. We choose to give maximum priority to Rijswijk residents for social rental homes.

122829.explanation: There is no place for coffeeshops in Rijswijk, not now or in the future. A coffeeshop does not fit the character of Rijswijk.

122832.explanation: Dividing houses into multiple units is harmful to the livability of Rijswijk neighborhoods. It leads to problems, causes too many people to live close together, and may lead to nuisances. Therefore, we oppose renting out single rooms.

122835.explanation: We want more parking spaces for cars and bicycles. We want more parking garages. By building parking garages for cars and bicycles, more space will be freed up in public areas.

122836.explanation: Rijswijk has multiple centers. These are already car-free. We want to keep them car-free.

122837.explanation: Nuisance is irritating and dangerous and must be tackled firmly. Police and public order officers intervene when rules are broken. In the Bogaard we see nuisance from vehicles such as fatbikes, scooters, and (electric) bicycles. Therefore, strict enforcement and fines are necessary.

122839.explanation: Funding for culture is used as effectively as possible. As a condition for subsidies, it is expected that the museum also earns its own money so that subsidies can be reduced. We support an entrepreneurial cultural sector that is largely self-sufficient.

122781.explanation: The CDA strives for a balanced male-female distribution, with suitability and quality always leading.

122782.explanation: The CDA supports broad participation and prior consultation. Referenda are not a suitable instrument for complex decision-making.

122785.explanation: The municipality must be able to continue balancing between tranquility and liveliness.

122788.explanation: The CDA believes that the Dispersal Act must be complied with.

122793.explanation: Waste processing and collection cost money. The disposal of bulky waste can be done a number of times free of charge. Only collection requires a contribution. Enforcement against illegal dumping must be improved.

122794.explanation: To prevent nuisance and protect the mostly young visitors, it is important to agree on standards about this in consultation with festivals and parties.

122795.explanation: The responsibility lies with entrepreneurs, with a facilitating role for the municipality.

122796.explanation: This is already happening in Rijswijk.

122799.explanation: This must be taken into account within poverty policy.

122801.explanation: Monuments are part of the character, history, and attractiveness of Rijswijk.

122802.explanation: Camera surveillance can help counteract nuisance and vandalism.

122804.explanation: They must be able to better protect themselves, and there is a deterrent effect.

122805.explanation: Because this causes an upward price effect on the price level of starter homes.

122808.explanation: Homes are meant to be lived in, but renting must remain possible for those who depend on it. A generic owner-occupancy obligation does not fit with this.

122811.explanation: Socially meaningful work helps people participate to the best of their ability and increases the chance of exiting social assistance.

122813.explanation: Exemption from taxes and the income supplement are for residents with an income at or around the social assistance level. This aligns well with what is considered too low an income in the Netherlands.

122815.explanation: There will be more electric cars, and charging must be easy.

122819.explanation: In healthcare, the patient's background is already taken into account where possible. Additional freedom of choice could unnecessarily increase costs.

122820.explanation: The municipality must pay attention to both quality and price. The number of providers to be contracted should be limited accordingly.

122821.explanation: This is already happening. It prevents period poverty and makes participation possible for everyone.

122822.explanation: The property tax (ozb) in Rijswijk is not disproportionately high compared to neighboring municipalities.

122824.explanation: A moderate increase is justifiable so that visitors contribute to the facilities they use.

122827.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit have the same rights and obligations as other residents. Like other residents, in emergencies an urgency declaration can be issued for them.

122829.explanation: A coffeeshop does not fit with the character of Rijswijk and increases pressure on livability, safety, and enforcement. A coffeeshop attracts unwanted customers.

122832.explanation: Room rentals are needed for students and starters. The municipality should focus on quality and nuisance, not ban everything.

122835.explanation: The parking policy and standards were recently established. In new construction, not every dwelling is entitled to its own nearby parking space. In existing neighborhoods, paid parking is introduced if there is clear parking nuisance. It is now important to first implement the policy and evaluate it.

122836.explanation: Car traffic increasingly does not fit in the compact center. Making it car-free increases safety and livability.

122837.explanation: Nuisance in De Bogaard is structural. Visible and consistent enforcement is needed to restore safety and livability.

122839.explanation: It is the only museum in Rijswijk and has international and local recognition.

122781.explanation: Promoting diversity in the executive board is certainly important, but an exact equal number of men and women in the board is, in our view, too rigid and too specific a criterion.

122782.explanation: For important issues that have a big impact on residents' lives, a referendum can be useful to gauge public opinion.

122785.explanation: If cafés and restaurants are allowed to determine their own opening hours, this will inevitably lead to more nuisance for nearby residents.

122788.explanation: DenkK-PGenR supports humane shelter for people fleeing unsafe situations in their home countries. Rijswijk must take responsibility and do its part, like other municipalities.

122795.explanation: We want an inclusive Rijswijk for everyone. That naturally means taking into account people with disabilities. Everyone deserves equal opportunities and everyone must be able to participate.

122796.explanation: People with less financial means must also have the opportunity to play sports. Sports connect people and are good for physical and mental well-being.

122799.explanation: Subsidizing music lessons for all primary school children is ineffective and too specific.

122801.explanation: We understand the value of monuments and the importance of monument care. Unfortunately, we also face other challenges requiring funding and choices must be made.

122802.explanation: We support more camera surveillance to enhance safety, with the important condition that attention is maintained for safeguarding residents' privacy.

122804.explanation: Violence against enforcement officers (boa's) has greatly increased. We cannot accept that. They are there to protect livability and safety in Rijswijk. Boa's must be able to perform their work safely and properly.

122805.explanation: As far as Denk-PgenR is concerned, there should be the possibility of a starter loan without interest.

122808.explanation: A home is meant to live in, not to be left empty or rented out for profit. In this housing crisis, there are many house seekers who, unfortunately, cannot manage to buy a home.

122818.explanation: We can only support the expansion of the environmental zone if (small) entrepreneurs can bear it. It is important to engage in timely conversations with entrepreneurs and provide more insight into the financial consequences for (small) businesses.

122819.explanation: We support an inclusive Rijswijk. It is important to be able to provide care that is appropriate for the client.

122820.explanation: It is primarily important that youth care is of good quality. Therefore, price must not be the most important factor when choosing youth care.

122827.explanation: The housing crisis is a big problem, but refugees are not the cause. We must build many more homes quickly. Status holders should have priority for social rental housing because otherwise they will never have a chance due to enormous waiting lists.

122829.explanation: Coffeeshops cause nuisance; moreover, we want to discourage drug use in Rijswijk.

122832.explanation: A ban on subdividing homes leads to higher rents and an even greater housing shortage.

122836.explanation: We believe that Rijswijk's center must be easily accessible for everyone, including by car.

122837.explanation: Street nuisance is a serious problem that creates a feeling of insecurity. We favor more enforcement against street nuisance.

122781.explanation: Every job must be filled based on competencies, not gender. Forum for Democracy is against all forms of discrimination, including so-called "positive" discrimination.

122782.explanation: Referenda are an important instrument to stimulate public debate and give citizens direct influence on major issues.

122785.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be facilitated by the municipality. Forum for Democracy wants to give entrepreneurs space.

122788.explanation: The asylum seekers' center on Lange Kleiweg must be closed, and Forum for Democracy will not accept new centers at other locations in Rijswijk.

122793.explanation: Collecting bulky waste is one of the tasks of a waste collection company, and we already pay them for their work through waste disposal charges. In Rijswijk, we even pay the highest waste disposal charges in the Netherlands. There should absolutely be no discussion about this.

122794.explanation: There are already legal standards; they do not need to be made stricter.

122795.explanation: This only costs money; where necessary, entrepreneurs can handle this themselves.

122796.explanation: Residents of Rijswijk should not have to pay the membership fees of a sports club on behalf of others.

122799.explanation: Forum for Democracy wants to reduce municipal expenditures, not increase them. Therefore, no additional subsidies. Schools are free to offer music lessons themselves.

122801.explanation: Monuments are important for our country and our municipality. They remind us of our past and we can learn from them. If our monuments are lost, we also lose our history, which we must try to avoid.

122802.explanation: Camera surveillance should be reviewed on a case-by-case basis, and privacy is an important right.

122804.explanation: Carrying a baton requires proper training, psychological evaluations, and entails great responsibility. That is what the police are for. We must handle the government’s monopoly on violence with great care.

122805.explanation: The municipality is a government institution, not a bank or mortgage provider. We want the municipality to focus on core tasks and leave things like loans to the appropriate institutions.

122808.explanation: Everyone should have the freedom to buy a home, regardless of whether they themselves intend to live in it. Buying a home as an investment or as a house for your child should always be possible.

122811.explanation: It's good if these people give something back to society. Additionally, it helps them maintain their connection with the community, from which the chance of finding a job is greater.

122813.explanation: More attention should be paid to disposable income with respect for the individual instead of shifting criteria.

122815.explanation: There is already a shortage of parking spaces and FVD opposes discriminating against regular motorists.

122818.explanation: Forum for Democracy wants Rijswijk to remain accessible to everyone. Residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs should always be able to come to our beautiful city.

122819.explanation: Forum for Democracy believes in the free choice of the individual.

122820.explanation: The municipality should focus on quality and added value for the citizens of Rijswijk.

122821.explanation: Providing tampons and sanitary pads is not a core task of the municipality. These products are available in many different places.

122822.explanation: The municipality should stick to its core tasks so that the tax burden becomes lighter. Property taxes are currently too high.

122824.explanation: It should be made as attractive as possible to visit Rijswijk. We owe that to our local entrepreneurs.

122827.explanation: The scarce social rental homes should primarily be available to Rijswijk residents who have been on the waiting list for years. Forum for Democracy will never agree to priority for immigrants.

122829.explanation: We are against excluding coffeeshops or other businesses. Every entrepreneur who wants to start something in Rijswijk is held to the same standards and must comply with rules and guidelines. If they do, they should be given the chance to establish their business.

122832.explanation: Homeowners should be free to decide what to do with their property. Moreover, we live in a time of housing shortage. Owners who convert and rent out their homes actually provide additional housing.

122835.explanation: There is a parking problem in our city. In the center, not only residents but also entrepreneurs are affected. Everyone’s mobility needs must be facilitated - this also applies to motorists, which requires sufficient parking spaces.

122836.explanation: Motorists are also welcome in the city center. We consider the accessibility of the city very important, and accessibility is not limited to public transport, cyclists, and pedestrians; motorists are an important part of that too.

122837.explanation: Those who cause nuisance must be dealt with appropriately. Such problems cannot be solved with kid gloves.

122839.explanation: Costs and benefits must be examined very critically. Decisions should be made based on that.

statements.title: Municipal enforcement officers should act more frequently and more forcefully against nuisance in Bogaard city centre.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: moeten vaker en dwingender optreden tegen overlast in Bogaard stadscentrum.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
There should be more camera surveillance in Rijswijk.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The residents of Rijswijk should be able to give their opinion on important matters through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Rijswijk moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Rijswijk should introduce an environmental zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the centre.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: The municipality must prevent the opening of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The centre of Rijswijk should be car-free.
The BOAs who monitor safety in Rijswijk should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Rijswijk controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Less money should go to the Rijswijk Museum.
